title: How my mother named me
summary: Lan's mother could not pick a name for her third child. Then a neighbour brought a small shy orchid that flowered on the day Lan was born.
body:
My name is Lan. It means 'orchid' in Vietnamese.

When my mother was pregnant, she did not have a name for me. She had a name for my brother. He was born first. She had a name for my older sister. But for me, the third child, she could not decide.

My mother says the name came in a strange way. The week before I was born, an old neighbour gave her a small orchid in a pot. The neighbour said, 'Take care of this. It is shy. It will not flower for a year.'

My mother put the orchid by the window. She watered it. She talked to it. She did not believe it would flower.

I was born on a Tuesday in October. The orchid flowered on the same Tuesday in October. There was one small white flower.

My mother says she did not plan to name me after the orchid. But when she looked at me, she said the word, and the word stayed.

The old neighbour came to visit. She saw the flower. She saw me. She smiled. She said, 'Now there are two.'

I am twelve now. I do not have an orchid in my room. They are too hard for me to grow. But every year, on my birthday, my mother brings me a small white flower. She does not say why.

I know why.

annotations.meaningVi: nhút nhát (ở đây dùng cho cây: chưa chịu nở hoa)
nở hoa (động từ); bông hoa (danh từ)
chăm sóc, trông nom (cụm động từ)
theo một cách kỳ lạ (cụm trạng ngữ chỉ cách thức)
cạnh/bên cửa sổ (giới từ chỉ vị trí)
đã tưới nước (động từ quá khứ: water = tưới)
không thể quyết định được (modal + động từ nguyên thể)
đặt tên tôi theo cây lan (name sb after sth = đặt tên ai theo điều gì đó)
đến thăm (came to + động từ: mục đích của hành động quá khứ)
từ đó đọng lại / được giữ nguyên (stay = ở lại, cố định)

annotations.phrase: too hard for me to grow
annotations.meaningVi: quá khó để tôi trồng (cấu trúc: too + adj + for sb + to-infinitive)
trong một cái chậu (chỉ vật dụng trồng cây)

grammarNotes.title: Linking past events with 'when' and 'before'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Trong văn kể, dùng 'when' và 'before' để liên kết các sự kiện. Trong bài: 'When my mother was pregnant, she did not have a name for me.' / 'The week before I was born, an old neighbour gave her a small orchid.' 'When' = vào lúc; 'before' = trước khi.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: In narrative, 'when' and 'before' link past events. From the story: 'When my mother was pregnant, she did not have a name for me.' / 'The week before I was born, an old neighbour gave her a small orchid.'

blanks.explanation: When her mother was pregnant — before Lan was born.
Lan is the third child — after her brother and older sister.
An old neighbour gave her mother the orchid.
The neighbour said the orchid is shy — it will not flower for a year.
She watered it — gave it water — and talked to it.
Lan was born on a Tuesday in October.
There was one small white flower — the orchid had flowered.
Every year on her birthday, her mother brings her a small white flower.
Her mother does not say why; Lan knows why — because of the orchid that flowered on the day she was born.

translationVi:
Tên tôi là Lan. Trong tiếng Việt nghĩa là 'hoa lan'.

Khi mẹ tôi đang mang thai, mẹ không có sẵn tên cho tôi. Mẹ đã có tên cho anh trai tôi — anh sinh trước. Mẹ đã có tên cho chị tôi. Nhưng với tôi, đứa con thứ ba, mẹ không quyết định được.

Mẹ nói cái tên đến một cách kỳ lạ. Tuần trước khi tôi sinh, một bà hàng xóm già đem cho mẹ một chậu lan nhỏ. Bà nói: 'Chăm nó nhé. Nó nhút nhát lắm. Một năm nữa mới có hoa đâu.'

Mẹ đặt chậu lan ở cửa sổ. Mẹ tưới. Mẹ trò chuyện với nó. Mẹ không tin nó sẽ nở.

Tôi sinh vào một ngày thứ Ba của tháng Mười. Chậu lan nở đúng vào ngày thứ Ba đó của tháng Mười. Có một bông hoa nhỏ màu trắng.

Mẹ nói mẹ không định đặt tên tôi theo cây lan. Nhưng khi mẹ nhìn tôi, mẹ buột miệng nói từ đó, và từ đó đọng lại.

Bà hàng xóm già sang chơi. Bà thấy bông hoa. Bà thấy tôi. Bà mỉm cười. Bà nói: 'Giờ là hai rồi.'

Tôi mười hai tuổi. Tôi không có cây lan trong phòng. Nó khó trồng quá với tôi. Nhưng mỗi năm, vào sinh nhật tôi, mẹ đem cho tôi một bông hoa nhỏ màu trắng. Mẹ không nói vì sao.

Tôi biết vì sao.

questions.prompt: What does Lan's name mean in Vietnamese?
questions.hint: Look at the very first lines.

questions.prompt: How many older brothers and sisters does Lan have?
options: One brother and one older sister
questions.explanation: She is the third child — after her brother (first) and older sister.

questions.prompt: What did the old neighbour bring before Lan was born?
options: A small orchid in a pot
questions.explanation: 'An old neighbour gave her a small orchid in a pot.'

questions.prompt: What did the neighbour say about the orchid?
options: It is shy and will not flower for a year
questions.explanation: 'It is shy. It will not flower for a year.'
'I was born on a Tuesday in October.'

questions.prompt: What happened to the orchid on the same day?
options: It flowered — one small white flower
questions.explanation: 'The orchid flowered on the same Tuesday in October. There was one small white flower.'

questions.prompt: Why did her mother name her Lan?
options: She had planned the name for years
She said the word when she looked at the baby — and it stayed
questions.explanation: 'When she looked at me, she said the word, and the word stayed.'

questions.prompt: What did the neighbour say when she saw the baby and the flower?
Why doesn't Lan have an orchid in her room?
options: Orchids are too hard for her to grow
Her mother does not let her
questions.explanation: 'They are too hard for me to grow.'

questions.prompt: Why does her mother bring her a small white flower every year?
options: Because of the orchid that flowered on the day Lan was born — even though her mother does not explain
questions.explanation: Lan knows why, even though her mother does not say.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Lan's name 'came in a strange way' — through a flower that opened on the day she was born. Think of how something in your life got its name (a pet, a corner of a room, a habit). Was the name planned, or did it 'arrive'?
